display_name: Reply to Legal Notice (on behalf of the Noticee)
structural_summary: A formal reply sent by or for the noticee answering a legal notice received from the opposite party or their advocate. Layout: replying party's/advocate's block; date; mode of dispatch (Registered Post A.D./email); addressee block (original notice sender or their advocate); subject line referencing the notice under reply with its date and reference number; WITHOUT PREJUDICE rubric; introduction recording receipt of the notice; preliminary objections terming the notice misconceived and untenable; para-wise reply specifically denying each adverse allegation; the noticee's true version of facts with documents relied on; counter-demands (withdrawal of the notice, costs); express reservation of all rights, civil and criminal; formal closing and signature block.
statutory_requirements:
- Identify the notice under reply precisely: its date, sender, sender's advocate (if any) and reference number where available.
- Mark the reply WITHOUT PREJUDICE and state it issues without prejudice to the noticee's rights and contentions (customary drafting practice, not a statutory mandate).
- Raise preliminary objections before the merits: e.g., the notice is false, frivolous, misconceived or untenable in law.
- Reply para-wise to the notice; deny each adverse allegation specifically (not by bare general denial); add the customary saving that nothing is admitted save what is specifically admitted.
- Set out the noticee's true version of facts with dates and the documents relied upon.
- Make counter-demands where appropriate: withdrawal of the notice and costs of the reply.
- Expressly reserve all rights and remedies, civil and criminal.
- Record the mode of dispatch.
- Statutory citations are generally unnecessary in a reply; cite an Act only where the underlying dispute requires it.
- Cheque-bounce-allegation replies may reference the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881 generally (e.g., absence of a legally enforceable debt); specific defence provisions [VERIFY: NI Act presumption/defence sections].
- Eviction replies may contest notice-period validity under the tenancy agreement and [VERIFY: Transfer of Property Act 1882 s.106 / applicable state rent legislation, e.g. Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999].
- Consumer replies answer the alleged deficiency in service or defect in goods; [VERIFY: Consumer Protection Act 2019 reference where the notice invokes it].

fields.name: sender_name
fields.description: The noticee on whose behalf the reply is sent

fields.name: original_sender_name
fields.description: Party who sent the legal notice being replied to

fields.name: notice_date
fields.description: Date of the received legal notice

fields.name: reply_date
fields.description: Date of dispatch of this reply; offset keeps it after the notice date and never in the future given the anchor range

fields.name: disputed_amount
fields.description: Amount demanded in the original notice and disputed in the reply

fields.name: addressee_address
fields.description: Address of the original notice sender or their advocate

fields.name: advocate_name
fields.placeholder: [NAME OF ADVOCATE FOR SENDER OF REPLY]

fields.name: original_advocate_name
fields.placeholder: [NAME OF ADVOCATE FOR ORIGINAL NOTICE SENDER]
fields.description: If the received notice was issued through counsel, the reply is addressed to this advocate

fields.name: notice_reference_number
fields.description: Outward/reference number quoted on the received notice
